meanings.definition: make changes for improvement in order to remove abuse and injustices
bring, lead, or force to abandon a wrong or evil course of life, conduct, and adopt a right one
improve by alteration or correction of errors or defects and put into a better condition
examples: reform the health system in this country
The lazy student promised to reform
the habitual cheater finally saw the light

meanings.definition: of or relating to the body of Protestant Christianity arising during the Reformation; used of some Protestant churches especially Calvinist as distinct from Lutheran
caused to abandon an evil manner of living and follow a good one
